claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) is caused by a label-encoding convention in which each label token is drawn from a closed vocabulary of fewer than 150 distinct words, and the type-token ratio for zodiac-section words is significantly lower than for any prose section (herbal, recipes, text-only).
evidence_for: Zodiac section has only 1,297 words across 12 folios â€” consistent with repetitive labeling
Low entropy is most parsimoniously explained by a small, frequently recycled label set
Astronomical section (872 words, entropy 3.7471) shows a similar pattern, suggesting label-driven encoding across diagram sections
High-probability beliefs [0.645, 0.478] already support label-oriented encoding in zodiac
evidence_against: Low entropy could also result from a polyalphabetic cipher that cycles with short period in label contexts
If labels were drawn from a truly closed vocabulary, unique word count for zodiac should be well under 150 â€” this can be falsified directly
The zodiac section could simply contain shorter words, which mechanically lowers entropy
test: Count distinct word types in the zodiac section; compute type-token ratio (TTR) for zodiac vs. herbal and recipes sections; compare average word length distributions across sections using a KS test.
test_metric: Zodiac TTR, zodiac unique word count, and KS statistic comparing zodiac vs. herbal word-length distributions
test_threshold: Confirm if zodiac unique word count <150 and zodiac TTR is at least 0.15 lower than herbal TTR; deny if zodiac unique word count >250 or TTR difference <0.05.

tests_run.test: count distinct word types in the zodiac section; compute type-token ratio (ttr) for zodiac vs. herba
tests_run.details: Observed avg word length: 5.03. Expected: ~5.0. Difference: 0.03.

reasoning: A confirmed label-vocabulary ceiling would let researchers treat zodiac tokens as a constrained ciphertext with known plaintext candidates (month names, star names, nymph labels), creating a potential crib for the broader cipher.
